method_atom: A numerical truncated-basis variational renormalization technique (a particle-hole formulation of the density-matrix renormalization group) for computing ground-state energetics, excitation gaps, and correlation observables of a finite assembly of many interacting units coupled by an attractive pairwise interaction. It is used to trace the smooth crossover, as the system size is varied, between a collective condensate-like ordered regime (large systems) and a fluctuation-dominated regime (small systems), with no critical separation between them. The results are benchmarked against an exact integrable solution and against a simplified mean-field-type variational ansatz whose norms and expectation values are evaluated by a new recursive algorithm.
object_atom: Ultrasmall metallic grains containing on the order of 20 to 400 conduction electrons, whose superconducting pairing gives rise to condensation energies, spectroscopic gaps, and BCS-like pairing parameters ranging from the bulk superconducting regime down to the few-electron limit.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the DMRG/exact-integrable-benchmark/mean-field-ansatz-with-recursive-evaluation machinery and the size-driven crossover between an ordered condensate and a fluctuation-dominated regime; domain-specific is that the interacting units are conduction electrons in nanoscale superconducting metallic grains.
